claim: A SETTLED requirement's last_reviewed_at shall be updated only after its claim/why/evidence/source_refs have actually been read and checked against current code/graph reality; a blanket date backfill with no substantive check is not a review and must not set last_reviewed_at.
why: This session's own history is the cautionary precedent. cmd/seed-due (removed, P2-2) backfilled last_reviewed_at/review_after on every SETTLED requirement from settled_at/created_at with no actual re-read of the text -- necessary to bootstrap the freshness engine (R-requirement-freshness-fields) with real dates instead of a universal NEVER-REVIEWED, but a review in name only: at the moment of backfill, 155+ of those requirements still described the superseded implementation (the P0-4 content-refresh wave that followed found and fixed exactly that staleness). A freshness field that can be set without reading the content it claims to attest defeats its own purpose -- hotam due would report those 155+ requirements as fresh while they were substantively wrong. This rule closes that gap going forward: a genuine review means confronting the current claim/why/evidence/source_refs against the real code or graph state (the same discipline the mediation loop's CONFRONT step already names) before touching last_reviewed_at, not merely rolling a date forward. Structurally unenforceable by design -- no mechanical check can verify a human or agent actually read and understood text, the same honesty boundary that sank R-boot-cite-measured (REJECTED, form-metric theatre) -- so this stays PROSE discipline, upheld by resolver review of what a ProposedReviewMark or ProposedRequirement UPDATE's evidence/why actually says, not by an automated check. RECLASSIFIED 2026-07-13: enforceability ENFORCEABLE -> INHERENTLY_PROSE (agent consultation, fm) -- this requirement's own text already argued unenforceability by design; the enforceability field is now brought in line with that argument, removing it from the closeable-debt count honestly rather than leaving it counted as fixable-but-unfixed.
